title: Alpha-beta-gamma diversity over time
text: Two parties dominate (SPÖ, ÖVP) steady shares across three decades. β sit near 1 every q.
code: austria <- rbind(   `1986` = c(SPOE = 0.437, OEVP = 0.421, FPOE = 0.098, Gruene = 0.044),   `1990` = c(SPOE = 0.437, OEVP = 0.328, FPOE = 0.180, Gruene = 0.055),   `1994` = c(SPOE = 0.355, OEVP = 0.284, FPOE = 0.235, Gruene = 0.071),   `1995` = c(SPOE = 0.388, OEVP = 0.350, FPOE = 0.224, Gruene = 0.049) ) alpha_beta_gamma(austria, q = c(0, 1, 2, 5),                  years = c(1986, 1990, 1994, 1995)) #> Warning: as_composition(): renormalizing rows so each sums to 1 (max deviation #> was 0.055). #> <beta_diversity: T = 4 rows, 4 q points> #>  q    alpha     beta    gamma T #>  0 4.000000 1.000000 4.000000 4 #>  1 3.296660 1.014637 3.344912 4 #>  2 3.004748 1.019008 3.061863 4 #>  5 2.713200 1.024388 2.779369 4

title: Alpha-beta-gamma diversity over time
text: 2002 2005 SPD CDU/CSU swap largest party. Column order stays constant (keeps identity); entries move. β₂ captures swap.
code: germany <- rbind(   `2002` = c(CDU = 0.315, CSU = 0.096, SPD = 0.416,              FDP = 0.078, Gruene = 0.091, Linke = 0.003),   `2005` = c(CDU = 0.293, CSU = 0.075, SPD = 0.362,              FDP = 0.099, Gruene = 0.083, Linke = 0.088),   `2009` = c(CDU = 0.312, CSU = 0.072, SPD = 0.235,              FDP = 0.150, Gruene = 0.109, Linke = 0.122) ) bd <- alpha_beta_gamma(germany, q = c(0, 1, 2, 5),                        years = c(2002, 2005, 2009)) #> Warning: as_composition(): renormalizing rows so each sums to 1 (max deviation #> was 0.001). bd #> <beta_diversity: T = 3 rows, 4 q points> #>  q    alpha     beta    gamma T #>  0 6.000000 1.000000 6.000000 3 #>  1 4.689208 1.040449 4.878880 3 #>  2 3.997689 1.039583 4.155931 3 #>  5 3.221741 1.067642 3.439665 3 plot(bd)

title: Alpha-beta-gamma diversity over time
text: make paper’s “sorting hides identity” point concrete: sort row descending, rerun decomposition. β collapses toward 1 pooled composition now averages “k-th largest slot” across rows, “share party X.” unsorted β q = 2 exceeds sorted one — exactly β_unsorted ≥ β_sorted inequality paper proves.
code: germany_sorted <- t(apply(germany, 1, sort, decreasing = TRUE)) colnames(germany_sorted) <- paste0("Rank_", seq_len(ncol(germany_sorted))) alpha_beta_gamma(germany_sorted, q = c(0, 1, 2, 5),                  years = c(2002, 2005, 2009)) #> Warning: as_composition(): renormalizing rows so each sums to 1 (max deviation #> was 0.001). #> <beta_diversity: T = 3 rows, 4 q points> #>  q    alpha     beta    gamma T #>  0 6.000000 1.000000 6.000000 3 #>  1 4.689208 1.025677 4.809613 3 #>  2 3.997689 1.020823 4.080931 3 #>  5 3.221741 1.033500 3.329670 3

title: Alpha-beta-gamma diversity over time
text: consecutive country-years repeat composition elections, pass years weight term length. alpha_beta_gamma() auto-collapses via collapse_to_elections().
code: germany_annual <- rbind(   `2002` = germany["2002", ],   `2003` = germany["2002", ],   # same composition   `2004` = germany["2002", ],   # same composition   `2005` = germany["2005", ],   `2006` = germany["2005", ],   `2007` = germany["2005", ],   `2008` = germany["2005", ],   `2009` = germany["2009", ] ) alpha_beta_gamma(germany_annual, q = 2, years = 2002:2009) #> Warning: as_composition(): renormalizing rows so each sums to 1 (max deviation #> was 0.001). #> <beta_diversity: T = 3 rows, 1 q points> #>  q    alpha     beta    gamma T #>  2 3.838372 1.023497 3.928561 3

title: Profile crossings: when ENP isn't enough
text: large comparisons, method = "signchange" cheap robust. rows draws asymmetric Dirichlet — stand-real party-labeled compositions; point row single party system, crossings() call treats row system without sorting assumption.
code: set.seed(1) many <- t(replicate(8, { x <- rgamma(5, 0.8); x / sum(x) })) colnames(many) <- paste0("Party_", LETTERS[1:5]) rownames(many) <- paste0("sys", 1:8) cr_all <- crossings(many, q = seq(0, 5, 0.01), method = "signchange") head(as.data.frame(cr_all)) #>   i j id_i id_j n_crossings first_q #> 1 1 2 sys1 sys2           0      NA #> 2 1 3 sys1 sys3           0      NA #> 3 1 4 sys1 sys4           1   0.055 #> 4 1 5 sys1 sys5           0      NA #> 5 1 6 sys1 sys6           0      NA #> 6 1 7 sys1 sys7           1   0.695

title: End-to-end with ParlGov data
text: parlgov_seat_shares() produces party-labeled matrix: one column per ParlGov party_id, preserved across elections. Rows country-year elections. row-sorting takes place; column 1 whichever party ParlGov lists first, “largest party year.” makes alpha_beta_gamma() actually detect changes effective power structure.
code: shares <- parlgov_seat_shares(pg,                               countries = c("Netherlands", "Sweden"),                               years     = 1960:2020,                               type      = "parliament") dim(shares) #> [1] 35 44

what: Pillar 1 — diversity profiles for a handful of elections
title: End-to-end with ParlGov data
code: pick <- c("Netherlands_1982", "Sweden_2002") plot(diversity_profile(shares[rownames(shares) %in% pick, , drop = FALSE]))

what: Pillar 2 — crossings between the two pick elections
title: End-to-end with ParlGov data
code: cr <- crossings(shares["Netherlands_1982", ],                 shares["Sweden_2002", ],                 q = seq(0, 5, 0.01)) cr #> <crossings: 1 pair, method = interp> #>  i j id_i id_j n_crossings   first_q #>  1 2    a    b           2 0.8559176 plot(cr)

what: Pillar 3 — beta diversity of one country’s trajectory
title: End-to-end with ParlGov data
code: nl <- shares[attr(shares, "country") == "Netherlands", , drop = FALSE] yr <- attr(shares, "year")[attr(shares, "country") == "Netherlands"] plot(alpha_beta_gamma(nl, q = c(0, 1, 2, 5), years = yr))

title: Getting started with partyscape
text: partyscape works party-labeled compositions: column specific party, preserved across elections. package deliberately ask row-sort shares. Sorting size makes “column 1” mean “largest party year,” loses identity information parties swap positions across elections — behaviour package’s alpha_beta_gamma() designed detect. Concretely: use named vectors (matrix named columns). entry order reflects party identity, rank. diversity_profile() sweeps q grid returns object print() / plot() / autoplot() methods:
code: germany_2009 <- c(CDU = 0.312, CSU = 0.072, SPD = 0.235,                   FDP = 0.150, Gruene = 0.109, Linke = 0.122)  hill_number(germany_2009, c(0, 1, 2, Inf)) #> [1] 6.000000 5.343992 4.830498 3.205128 enp(germany_2009) #> [1] 4.830498 dominance_gap(germany_2009) #>   CDU  #> 0.077 top_two(germany_2009) #> [1] 0.547 dp <- diversity_profile(germany_2009) plot(dp)

what: §1 What PCA on sorted compositional data rediscovers
title: Replicating the paper with partyscape
text: paper’s headline: first two PCs explain 87% variance; Monte Carlo nulls (uniform Dirichlet, matched-N0N_0 Dirichlet, Taagepera–Allik Dirichlet) reproduce qualitative loading shape, showing eigenvectors method-imposed, substantive.
code: pca_real      <- prcomp(X, center = TRUE, scale. = FALSE) real_var      <- summary(pca_real)$importance[2, 1:5] * 100 round(real_var, 1) cat(sprintf("PC1 + PC2 = %.1f%%\n", sum(real_var[1:2])))

previous_headings: §1 What PCA on sorted compositional data rediscovers
title: Replicating the paper with partyscape
text: taagepera_allik_shares() comes package; rest base R. Paper’s reference numbers: uniform null ≈ 60.3% / 18.6% variance, |r|≈0.88|r| \approx 0.88 (PC1) / 0.660.66 (PC2); matched-N0N_0 Taagepera–Allik nulls correlate near 1 real loadings.
code: n_sim    <- 50                 # bump to 500 for paper-exact numbers D        <- ncol(X) Ncy      <- nrow(X) N0_real  <- rowSums(X > 0) alpha_CH <- 39.1  sim_types <- c("Uniform Dirichlet",                "Matched-N0 Dirichlet",                "Taagepera-Allik Dirichlet")  sim_results <- setNames(vector("list", length(sim_types)), sim_types) for (type in sim_types) {   sim_results[[type]] <- list(     var1 = numeric(n_sim), var2 = numeric(n_sim),     L1 = matrix(0, n_sim, D), L2 = matrix(0, n_sim, D)   ) }  for (s in seq_len(n_sim)) {   # (A) Uniform Dirichlet, sorted   Xu <- matrix(rexp(Ncy * D, 1), Ncy, D); Xu <- Xu / rowSums(Xu)   Xu <- t(apply(Xu, 1, sort, decreasing = TRUE))    # (B) Matched-N_0 Dirichlet   Xm <- matrix(0, Ncy, D)   for (i in seq_len(Ncy)) {     n0 <- N0_real[i]     d  <- rexp(n0, 1); d <- sort(d / sum(d), decreasing = TRUE)     Xm[i, 1:n0] <- d   }    # (C) Taagepera-Allik (Cohen & Hanretty 2024, alpha = 39.1)   Xt <- matrix(0, Ncy, D)   for (i in seq_len(Ncy)) {     n0    <- N0_real[i]     p_loc <- taagepera_allik_shares(n0)            # <- package helper     theta <- alpha_CH * p_loc     d     <- rgamma(n0, shape = theta, rate = 1)     d     <- sort(d / sum(d), decreasing = TRUE)     Xt[i, 1:n0] <- d   }    for (k in seq_along(sim_types)) {     Xs   <- switch(k, Xu, Xm, Xt)     pp   <- prcomp(Xs, center = TRUE, scale. = FALSE)     sim_results[[k]]$var1[s] <- summary(pp)$importance[2, 1] * 100     sim_results[[k]]$var2[s] <- summary(pp)$importance[2, 2] * 100     sim_results[[k]]$L1[s, ] <- pp$rotation[, 1]     sim_results[[k]]$L2[s, ] <- pp$rotation[, 2]   } }  # Sign-align: PC1 entry 1 positive, PC2 entry 1 negative real_load <- pca_real$rotation[, 1:2] if (real_load[1, 1] < 0) real_load[, 1] <- -real_load[, 1] if (real_load[1, 2] > 0) real_load[, 2] <- -real_load[, 2] for (type in sim_types) for (s in seq_len(n_sim)) {   if (sim_results[[type]]$L1[s, 1] < 0)     sim_results[[type]]$L1[s, ] <- -sim_results[[type]]$L1[s, ]   if (sim_results[[type]]$L2[s, 1] > 0)     sim_results[[type]]$L2[s, ] <- -sim_results[[type]]$L2[s, ] }  mc_diag <- data.frame(   null = sim_types,   var1 = sapply(sim_results, function(x) mean(x$var1)),   sd1  = sapply(sim_results, function(x) sd(x$var1)),   var2 = sapply(sim_results, function(x) mean(x$var2)),   sd2  = sapply(sim_results, function(x) sd(x$var2)),   abs_r_PC1 = sapply(sim_results, function(x)     mean(abs(apply(x$L1, 1, cor, real_load[, 1])))),   abs_r_PC2 = sapply(sim_results, function(x)     mean(abs(apply(x$L2, 1, cor, real_load[, 2])))) ) round(mc_diag[, -1], 3)

title: Replicating the paper with partyscape
code: df_fig1 <- data.frame() df_fig1 <- rbind(df_fig1, data.frame(   party   = rep(1:D, 2),   loading = c(real_load[, 1], real_load[, 2]),   lower   = NA, upper = NA,   source  = "Real data (Magyar)",   pc      = rep(c("PC1", "PC2"), each = D) )) for (type in sim_types) {   m1  <- colMeans(sim_results[[type]]$L1)   ci1 <- apply(sim_results[[type]]$L1, 2, quantile, c(0.025, 0.975))   m2  <- colMeans(sim_results[[type]]$L2)   ci2 <- apply(sim_results[[type]]$L2, 2, quantile, c(0.025, 0.975))   df_fig1 <- rbind(df_fig1, data.frame(     party = rep(1:D, 2),     loading = c(m1, m2),     lower   = c(ci1[1, ], ci2[1, ]),     upper   = c(ci1[2, ], ci2[2, ]),     source  = paste0(type, " (95% CI)"),     pc      = rep(c("PC1", "PC2"), each = D)   )) }  ggplot(df_fig1, aes(party, loading, colour = source, fill = source)) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "grey70") +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), alpha = 0.15, colour = NA) +   geom_line() + geom_point(size = 0.8) +   facet_wrap(~pc) +   scale_colour_manual(values = partyscape_palette(4)) +   scale_fill_manual(values   = partyscape_palette(4), guide = "none") +   labs(x = "Party rank", y = "Loading weight", colour = NULL) +   theme_bw(base_size = 10) +   theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.direction = "vertical")

what: Closure constraint and sorting-induced variance concentration (§A3)
title: Replicating the paper with partyscape
code: cov_X  <- cov(X) cat(sprintf("max |rowSums(Sigma)| = %.3e  (closure)\n",             max(abs(rowSums(cov_X))))) col_vars <- apply(X, 2, var) round(col_vars[1:5], 4) cat(sprintf("Var(x_(1)) / total = %.3f  (paper: 0.453)\n",             col_vars[1] / sum(col_vars)))

previous_headings: §2 PC1 and the effective number of parties
title: Replicating the paper with partyscape
text: Sub-PCA (ztop,zgap)(z_\text{top}, z_\text{gap}) alone predicts full-PCA PC1 R2=0.991R^2 = 0.991 PC2 R2=0.993R^2 = 0.993.
code: Z     <- cbind(z_top, z_gap) pca_Z <- prcomp(Z, center = TRUE, scale. = FALSE) sub   <- pca_Z$x r2_pc1_sub <- max(summary(lm(pc1 ~ sub[, 1]))$r.squared,                   summary(lm(pc1 ~ sub[, 2]))$r.squared) r2_pc2_sub <- max(summary(lm(pca_real$x[, 2] ~ sub[, 1]))$r.squared,                   summary(lm(pca_real$x[, 2] ~ sub[, 2]))$r.squared) c(R2_full_PC1_from_2D = r2_pc1_sub,   R2_full_PC2_from_2D = r2_pc2_sub) |> round(4)

what: Σ₁₂ and the (A,B,C)(A, B, C) triple (§A4)
title: Replicating the paper with partyscape
code: S11 <- cov_X[1, 1]; S22 <- cov_X[2, 2]; S12 <- cov_X[1, 2] A_var <- S11 + 2 * S12 + S22          # Var(z_top) B_var <- S11 - 2 * S12 + S22          # Var(z_gap) C_cov <- S11 - S22                    # Cov(z_top, z_gap) c(Sigma11 = S11, Sigma22 = S22, Sigma12 = S12,   A = A_var, B = B_var, C = C_cov,   cor_top_gap = C_cov / sqrt(A_var * B_var)) |> round(5)

title: Replicating the paper with partyscape
code: eig <- eigen(cov(Z)) V   <- eig$vectors # Sign-align so the heavy component of each eigenvector is positive for (k in 1:2) {   heavy <- which.max(abs(V[, k]))   if (V[heavy, k] < 0) V[, k] <- -V[, k] } round(V, 3) round(eig$values, 5)

title: Replicating the paper with partyscape
text: hill_number() work; correlations sit 0.10 ≤3a \le 3 rise ≈0.20\approx 0.20 =10a = 10.
code: pc2 <- pca_real$x[, 2] q_probe <- c(0, 0.5, 1, 1.5, 2, 3, 5, 10) Na_mat <- sapply(q_probe, function(q) apply(X, 1, hill_number, q = q)) colnames(Na_mat) <- paste0("N_", q_probe) round(cor(pc2, Na_mat), 3)  cat(sprintf("R^2(PC2 ~ gap)         = %.3f\n",             summary(lm(pc2 ~ dominance_gap(X)))$r.squared)) cat(sprintf("cor(ENP, z_top)        = %.3f  (paper: 0.96)\n",             cor(enp_real, z_top)))

what: Figure: NaN_a level sets for a 3-party system
title: Replicating the paper with partyscape
text: Every scalar plot hill_number() call.
code: Na_3p <- function(x1, x2, a) hill_number(c(x1, x2, 1 - x1 - x2), a)  x1_ref <- 0.45; x2_ref <- 0.30 a_grid <- c(0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 10) ref_Na <- sapply(a_grid, function(a) Na_3p(x1_ref, x2_ref, a))  g <- expand.grid(x1 = seq(0.34, 0.65, length.out = 200),                  x2 = seq(0.05, 0.50, length.out = 200)) g$x3 <- 1 - g$x1 - g$x2 g    <- g[g$x1 >= g$x2 & g$x2 >= g$x3 & g$x3 >= 0, ]  levels_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(a_grid), function(k) {   a <- a_grid[k]   data.frame(g, Na = mapply(Na_3p, g$x1, g$x2, MoreArgs = list(a = a)),              a = factor(paste0("a = ", a), levels = paste0("a = ", a_grid))) }))  ggplot(levels_df, aes(x1, x2, z = Na, colour = a)) +   geom_contour(aes(group = a),                breaks = ref_Na[match(as.numeric(sub("a = ", "", levels(levels_df$a))),                                      a_grid)],                linewidth = 0.7) +   geom_point(data = data.frame(x1 = x1_ref, x2 = x2_ref),              aes(x1, x2), inherit.aes = FALSE, size = 3) +   scale_colour_manual(values = partyscape_palette(length(a_grid))) +   coord_fixed() +   labs(x = expression(x[(1)]), y = expression(x[(2)]),        colour = "level set",        title = "Level sets of N_a through A = (0.45, 0.30, 0.25)") +   theme_bw(base_size = 11)

title: Replicating the paper with partyscape
code: uk97  <- X["United Kingdom_1997", ] ger09 <- X["Germany_2009", ]  scalars <- data.frame(   N0     = c(sum(uk97 > 0),           sum(ger09 > 0)),   ENP    = c(enp(uk97),               enp(ger09)),   N_inf  = c(1 / max(uk97),           1 / max(ger09)),   gap    = c(dominance_gap(uk97),     dominance_gap(ger09)),   topTwo = c(top_two(uk97),           top_two(ger09)) ) rownames(scalars) <- c("UK 1997", "Germany 2009") round(scalars, 3)  dp_pair <- diversity_profile(rbind(`UK 1997` = uk97,                                    `Germany 2009` = ger09),                              q = seq(0, 8, 0.02)) autoplot(dp_pair) +   labs(title = "UK 1997 (steep — concentrated) vs. Germany 2009 (flat — balanced)")

title: Replicating the paper with partyscape
code: q_fine  <- seq(0, 10, 0.02) all_enp <- enp(X) all_gap <- dominance_gap(X)  select_16cy_kmeans <- function(enp_vec, gap_vec, k = 16, seed = 42) {   feats <- cbind(scale(enp_vec), scale(gap_vec))   set.seed(seed)   km <- kmeans(feats, centers = k, nstart = 50, iter.max = 100)   sel <- integer(k)   for (cl in seq_len(k)) {     members <- which(km$cluster == cl)     d <- rowSums(sweep(feats[members, , drop = FALSE], 2,                        km$centers[cl, ])^2)     sel[cl] <- members[which.min(d)]   }   sort(unique(sel)) } sel_16 <- select_16cy_kmeans(all_enp, all_gap) data.frame(country_year = rownames(X)[sel_16],            ENP = round(all_enp[sel_16], 2),            gap = round(all_gap[sel_16], 3)) M16    <- X[sel_16, , drop = FALSE] cr_16  <- crossings(M16, q = q_fine, method = "signchange") n_cross_16 <- sum(cr_16$pairs$n_crossings > 0) cat(sprintf("16-CY sample: %d of %d pairs cross (%.1f%%)  [paper: 44/120 = 36.7%%]\n",             n_cross_16, nrow(cr_16$pairs),             100 * n_cross_16 / nrow(cr_16$pairs)))

previous_headings: §6 Crossing diversity profiles > 16-country-year illustrative sample (§6 paragraph 1)
title: Replicating the paper with partyscape
code: tbl <- cr_16$pairs tbl$system_A <- rownames(M16)[tbl$i] tbl$system_B <- rownames(M16)[tbl$j] tbl$ENP_A    <- round(all_enp[sel_16][tbl$i], 2) tbl$ENP_B    <- round(all_enp[sel_16][tbl$j], 2) tbl$dENP     <- round(abs(all_enp[sel_16][tbl$i] -                            all_enp[sel_16][tbl$j]), 2) tbl$dgap     <- round(abs(all_gap[sel_16][tbl$i] -                            all_gap[sel_16][tbl$j]), 3) tbl$N0_A     <- rowSums(M16 > 0)[tbl$i] tbl$N0_B     <- rowSums(M16 > 0)[tbl$j]  cross_pairs <- tbl[tbl$n_crossings > 0, ] cross_pairs <- cross_pairs[order(cross_pairs$dENP), ] head(cross_pairs[, c("system_A", "system_B", "N0_A", "N0_B",                      "ENP_A", "ENP_B", "dENP", "dgap",                      "n_crossings", "first_q")], 10)

title: Replicating the paper with partyscape
text: 100 draws 4$$4 (ENP, gap) quartile grid. Paper: mean 33%, 5th–95th percentile 22%–44%.
code: stratified_redraw_rate <- function(enp_vec, gap_vec, n_draws = 100,                                    seed = 2026) {   enp_q <- quantile(enp_vec, seq(0, 1, length.out = 5))   gap_q <- quantile(gap_vec, seq(0, 1, length.out = 5))   set.seed(seed)   rates <- numeric(n_draws)   for (d in seq_len(n_draws)) {     sel <- integer(0)     for (i in 1:4) for (j in 1:4) {       in_cell <- enp_vec >= enp_q[i] & enp_vec <= enp_q[i + 1] &                  gap_vec >= gap_q[j] & gap_vec <= gap_q[j + 1]       idx <- which(in_cell)       if (length(idx)) sel <- c(sel, sample(idx, 1))     }     if (length(sel) < 16) {       sel <- c(sel, sample(setdiff(seq_along(enp_vec), sel),                            16 - length(sel)))     } else if (length(sel) > 16) sel <- sample(sel, 16)     cr <- crossings(X[sel, , drop = FALSE], q = q_fine,                     method = "signchange")     rates[d] <- mean(cr$pairs$n_crossings > 0)   }   rates }  redraw <- stratified_redraw_rate(all_enp, all_gap, n_draws = 100) c(mean     = mean(redraw),   p5       = quantile(redraw, 0.05),   p95      = quantile(redraw, 0.95),   min      = min(redraw),   max      = max(redraw)) |> round(3)

title: Replicating the paper with partyscape
text: compute full profile matrix diversity_profile() pull wide attribute fast sign-change scanning. Paper’s ladder: 52% → 79% across |Δzgap||\Delta z_{\mathrm{gap}}| bands.
code:
dp_full <- diversity_profile(X, q = q_fine) prof_mat <- attr(dp_full, "wide")  count_crossings_row <- function(pi, pj) {   d  <- pi - pj   nz <- d != 0   d  <- d[nz]   if (length(d) < 2) return(list(n = 0L, first = NA_real_))   s  <- sign(d)   ch <- which(diff(s) != 0)   list(n = length(ch),        first = if (length(ch)) q_fine[nz][ch[1]] else NA_real_) }  pairs_df <- data.frame() for (i in 1:(Ncy - 1L)) {   cand <- (i + 1L):Ncy   keep <- abs(all_enp[i] - all_enp[cand]) < 0.5   cand <- cand[keep]   for (j in cand) {     r <- count_crossings_row(prof_mat[i, ], prof_mat[j, ])     pairs_df <- rbind(pairs_df, data.frame(       i = i, j = j,       dENP = abs(all_enp[i] - all_enp[j]),       dgap = abs(all_gap[i] - all_gap[j]),       n_cross = r$n,       first_q = r$first     ))   } }  cat(sprintf("Near-ENP pairs (|dENP| < 0.5): %d  [paper: 53,861]
",             nrow(pairs_df))) cat(sprintf("Pairs with >=1 crossing:        %d (%.1f%%)  [paper: 66%%]
",             sum(pairs_df$n_cross > 0),             100 * mean(pairs_df$n_cross > 0))) cat(sprintf("Mean first-crossing q:          %.2f  [paper: 1.98]
",             mean(pairs_df$first_q, na.rm = TRUE)))  for (b in list(c(0, 0.05), c(0.05, 0.10), c(0.10, 0.15), c(0.15, 1))) {   m <- pairs_df$dgap >= b[1] & pairs_df$dgap < b[2]   cat(sprintf("  |dgap| in [%.2f, %.2f): n = %5d, rate = %.3f
",               b[1], b[2], sum(m), mean(pairs_df$n_cross[m] > 0))) }

%s vs %s

",               pp[1], pp[2],               enp(X[pp[1], ]), enp(X[pp[2], ]),               sum(X[pp[1], ] > 0), sum(X[pp[2], ] > 0),               1 / max(X[pp[1], ]), 1 / max(X[pp[2], ]),               if (cr$pairs$n_crossings == 0) "(none)"               else paste(round(cr$locations[[1]], 3), collapse = ", "))) }  plots <- lapply(case_pairs, function(pp) {   cr <- crossings(X[pp[1], ], X[pp[2], ], q = q_fine)   autoplot(cr) +     ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = 2, linetype = "dashed",                         colour = "grey50") +     ggplot2::labs(title = paste(pp[1], "vs", pp[2])) }) for (g in plots) print(g)

title: Replicating the paper with partyscape
text: alpha_beta_gamma() years = ... auto-collapses repeated consecutive rows via collapse_to_elections() weights term length. Key countries cited text: Unit-test (disjoint communities give β2=2\beta_2 = 2):
code: countries <- unique(dat$country) q_beta    <- c(0, 1, 2, 5)  beta_sorted <- do.call(rbind, lapply(countries, function(cc) {   idx <- which(dat$country == cc)   if (length(idx) < 3) return(NULL)   bd  <- alpha_beta_gamma(X[idx, , drop = FALSE],                           q = q_beta, years = dat$year[idx],                           collapse = TRUE)   data.frame(country = cc, q = bd$q, alpha = bd$alpha,              beta = bd$beta, gamma = bd$gamma, T = bd$T) }))  # Tidy wide table, ordered by beta at q = 0 (Figure 3's ordering) wide_sorted <- reshape(   beta_sorted[, c("country", "q", "beta")],   idvar = "country", timevar = "q", direction = "wide" ) names(wide_sorted) <- c("country", paste0("beta_q", q_beta)) wide_sorted <- wide_sorted[order(-wide_sorted$beta_q0), ] rownames(wide_sorted) <- NULL knitr::kable(wide_sorted, digits = 3,              caption = "Sorted-data beta by order (paper's Figure 3b).") for (cc in c("Greece", "Austria", "Italy", "Finland", "United Kingdom")) {   row <- wide_sorted[wide_sorted$country == cc, ]   cat(sprintf("  %-15s  beta_q0 = %.3f  beta_q2 = %.3f  beta_q5 = %.3f\n",               cc, row$beta_q0, row$beta_q2, row$beta_q5)) } disjoint <- rbind(c(rep(0.1, 10), rep(0, 10)),                   c(rep(0, 10), 0.75, 0.25, rep(0, 8))) tst <- alpha_beta_gamma(disjoint, q = 2) stopifnot(abs(tst$beta - 2) < 1e-8) cat(sprintf("Disjoint test: gamma=%.3f  alpha=%.3f  beta=%.3f  (expect 5.517, 2.759, 2.000)\n",             tst$gamma, tst$alpha, tst$beta))

title: Replicating the paper with partyscape
text: Italy’s headline numbers: Paper reports β0=3.87\beta_0 = 3.87, β2=2.35\beta_2 = 2.35, β5=2.09\beta_5 = 2.09.
code: parlgov_names <- c("Austria", "Belgium", "Denmark", "Finland", "France",                    "Germany", "Greece", "Iceland", "Ireland", "Italy",                    "Luxembourg", "Netherlands", "Norway", "Portugal",                    "Spain", "Sweden", "United Kingdom")  pg <- tryCatch(fetch_parlgov(), error = function(e) {   message("fetch_parlgov() failed (", conditionMessage(e),           "); falling back to local CSV.")   read.csv(parlgov_local, stringsAsFactors = FALSE) })  # The live endpoint uses full country names ("Germany"); the Magyar # replication CSV uses 3-letter ISO codes ("DEU"). Normalize to full # names so the rest of the chunk works regardless of source. iso_to_name <- c(AUT = "Austria", BEL = "Belgium", DNK = "Denmark",                  FIN = "Finland", FRA = "France", DEU = "Germany",                  GRC = "Greece",  ISL = "Iceland", IRL = "Ireland",                  ITA = "Italy",   LUX = "Luxembourg",                  NLD = "Netherlands", NOR = "Norway", PRT = "Portugal",                  ESP = "Spain",   SWE = "Sweden",                  GBR = "United Kingdom") if (all(nchar(unique(pg$country)) == 3L)) {   pg$country <- unname(iso_to_name[pg$country])   pg <- pg[!is.na(pg$country), ] }  pg  <- pg[pg$country %in% parlgov_names, ] pg  <- pg[!is.na(pg$seats), ] pg$year <- as.integer(substr(pg$date, 1, 4)) pg  <- pg[pg$year >= min(dat$year) & pg$year <= max(dat$year), ]  shares <- parlgov_seat_shares(pg,                               countries = parlgov_names,                               years     = min(dat$year):max(dat$year),                               type      = "parliament") dim(shares) country_vec <- attr(shares, "country") year_vec    <- attr(shares, "year")  beta_pg <- do.call(rbind, lapply(parlgov_names, function(cc) {   keep <- country_vec == cc   if (sum(keep) < 3) return(NULL)   mat <- shares[keep, , drop = FALSE]   # drop party columns that are zero throughout this country's history   mat <- mat[, colSums(mat) > 0, drop = FALSE]   bd  <- alpha_beta_gamma(mat, q = q_beta, years = year_vec[keep])   data.frame(country = cc, q = bd$q, alpha = bd$alpha,              beta = bd$beta, gamma = bd$gamma, T = bd$T) }))  wide_pg <- reshape(   beta_pg[, c("country", "q", "beta")],   idvar = "country", timevar = "q", direction = "wide" ) names(wide_pg) <- c("country", paste0("beta_q", q_beta)) wide_pg <- wide_pg[order(-wide_pg$beta_q0), ] rownames(wide_pg) <- NULL knitr::kable(wide_pg, digits = 3,              caption = "Unsorted (ParlGov) beta by order (paper's Figure 3).") beta_pg[beta_pg$country == "Italy", ]

title: Replicating the paper with partyscape
code: cmp <- merge(beta_sorted[, c("country", "q", "beta")],              beta_pg[,     c("country", "q", "beta")],              by = c("country", "q"),              suffixes = c(".sorted", ".parlgov")) cmp_q2 <- cmp[cmp$q == 2, ] cmp_q2 <- cmp_q2[order(cmp_q2$beta.parlgov), ] cmp_q2$country <- factor(cmp_q2$country, levels = cmp_q2$country)  ggplot(cmp_q2) +   geom_segment(aes(x = beta.sorted, xend = beta.parlgov,                    y = country, yend = country),                colour = "grey70") +   geom_point(aes(x = beta.sorted, y = country,                  colour = "sorted (Magyar)"), size = 2) +   geom_point(aes(x = beta.parlgov, y = country,                  colour = "unsorted (ParlGov)"), size = 2) +   geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey60") +   scale_colour_manual(values = partyscape_palette(2)) +   labs(x = expression(beta(q == 2)), y = NULL, colour = NULL) +   theme_bw(base_size = 11)

title: Replicating the paper with partyscape
code: q_grid <- seq(0, 5, 0.1)  profile_one <- function(mat, years) {   bd <- alpha_beta_gamma(mat, q = q_grid, years = years)   bd$beta }  bp_sorted <- do.call(rbind, lapply(countries, function(cc) {   idx <- which(dat$country == cc)   if (length(idx) < 3) return(NULL)   data.frame(country = cc, q = q_grid,              beta = profile_one(X[idx, , drop = FALSE], dat$year[idx]),              panel = "Sorted (Magyar)") }))  bp_pg <- do.call(rbind, lapply(parlgov_names, function(cc) {   keep <- country_vec == cc   if (sum(keep) < 3) return(NULL)   mat <- shares[keep, , drop = FALSE]   mat <- mat[, colSums(mat) > 0, drop = FALSE]   data.frame(country = cc, q = q_grid,              beta = profile_one(mat, year_vec[keep]),              panel = "Unsorted (ParlGov)") }))  bp <- rbind(bp_sorted, bp_pg) hi <- c("Italy", "Belgium", "Greece", "Austria") bp$colour <- ifelse(bp$country %in% hi, bp$country, "(other)")  ggplot(bp, aes(q, beta, group = country, colour = colour)) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey60") +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.6) +   facet_wrap(~ panel, scales = "free_y") +   scale_colour_manual(values = c(     Italy   = partyscape_palette(4)[1],     Belgium = partyscape_palette(4)[2],     Greece  = partyscape_palette(4)[3],     Austria = partyscape_palette(4)[4],     `(other)` = "grey75")) +   labs(x = "order q", y = expression(beta(q)), colour = NULL) +   theme_bw(base_size = 11)

title: Diversity Profiles, Crossings, and Beta Diversity for Party Systems
text: examples work party-labeled compositions — columns carry party identity, never rank. paper’s central point sorting shares size (“column 1 = largest party year”) discards information alpha_beta_gamma() designed detect, package encourages keep party identity intact.
code: library(partyscape)  # Germany 2009 Bundestag — columns named by party, not sorted by size. germany_2009 <- c(   CDU   = 0.312, CSU   = 0.072, SPD   = 0.235,   FDP   = 0.150, Gruene = 0.109, Linke = 0.122 )  # UK 1997 Commons — a different party set, also named (not sorted). uk_1997 <- c(   Labour       = 0.634, Conservative = 0.250, LibDem   = 0.070,   UUP          = 0.015, SNP          = 0.009, PlaidCymru = 0.006,   SDLP         = 0.005, DUP          = 0.003, SinnFein = 0.003,   Independent  = 0.005 )  # 1. Diversity profile: a steep UK curve vs. a flat German one. diversity_profile(uk_1997) #> <diversity_profile: 1 composition over q grid [0, 5], 101 points> #>   D(0)  D(1)  D(2)  D(5) #> 1   10 2.845 2.129 1.763 diversity_profile(germany_2009) #> <diversity_profile: 1 composition over q grid [0, 5], 101 points> #>   D(0)  D(1) D(2)  D(5) #> 1    6 5.344 4.83 4.029  # 2. Profile crossings across two systems. crossings(uk_1997, germany_2009, q = seq(0, 5, 0.01)) #> <crossings: 1 pair, method = interp> #>  i j id_i id_j n_crossings   first_q #>  1 2    a    b           1 0.3178071  # 3. Beta diversity across Germany's 2002-2005-2009 trajectory. Columns # share the same party slots across rows so CDU/SPD swapping as the # largest party between 2002 and 2005 is visible to the decomposition. germany <- rbind(   `2002` = c(CDU = 0.315, CSU = 0.096, SPD = 0.416,              FDP = 0.078, Gruene = 0.091, Linke = 0.003),   `2005` = c(CDU = 0.293, CSU = 0.075, SPD = 0.362,              FDP = 0.099, Gruene = 0.083, Linke = 0.088),   `2009` = c(CDU = 0.312, CSU = 0.072, SPD = 0.235,              FDP = 0.150, Gruene = 0.109, Linke = 0.122) ) alpha_beta_gamma(germany, q = c(0, 1, 2, 5),                  years = c(2002, 2005, 2009)) #> Warning: as_composition(): renormalizing rows so each sums to 1 (max deviation #> was 0.001). #> <beta_diversity: T = 3 rows, 4 q points> #>  q    alpha     beta    gamma T #>  0 6.000000 1.000000 6.000000 3 #>  1 4.689208 1.040449 4.878880 3 #>  2 3.997689 1.039583 4.155931 3 #>  5 3.221741 1.067642 3.439665 3

what: Alpha, beta, and gamma diversity (Jost 2007) — alpha_beta_gamma
title: Alpha, beta, and gamma diversity (Jost 2007) — alpha_beta_gamma
text: Multiplicative decomposition Hill-number diversity across rows composition matrix. Alpha generalized mean order (1 - q) per-row Hill numbers (Jost 2007, Eqs. 11a–11b), gamma Hill number (possibly weighted) pooled composition, beta gamma / alpha. beta bounded [1, T] reads "effective number structurally distinct compositions" across T rows. Vectorized q: one call returns full beta profile.

title: Alpha, beta, and gamma diversity (Jost 2007) — alpha_beta_gamma
code: alpha_beta_gamma(   mat,   q = c(0, 1, 2, 5),   w = NULL,   years = NULL,   collapse = !is.null(years) )

title: Alpha, beta, and gamma diversity (Jost 2007) — alpha_beta_gamma
text: mat numeric matrix non-negative shares (rows = time points / systems; cols = parties). Accepts anything as_composition() accepts. q numeric vector orders. Default c(0, 1, 2, 5). w optional weights (numeric, length = T, summing 1). NULL, equal weights used — term lengths collapse = TRUE years supplied. years optional integer vector election years aligned mat. Enables collapsing identical consecutive rows. collapse logical; defaults !.null(years).

title: Alpha, beta, and gamma diversity (Jost 2007) — alpha_beta_gamma
text: S3 "beta_diversity" object — data.frame columns q, alpha, beta, gamma, T. Attributes carry weights collapse info.

title: Alpha, beta, and gamma diversity (Jost 2007) — alpha_beta_gamma
text: years supplied (collapse = TRUE), consecutive identical rows collapsed via collapse_to_elections() unique composition counted ; weights default term lengths long-lived elections dominate.

title: Alpha, beta, and gamma diversity (Jost 2007) — alpha_beta_gamma
code: # Three German Bundestag elections. Columns are stable party slots; # SPD and CDU/CSU swap positions as the largest party between 2002 # (SPD) and 2005/2009 (CDU/CSU). Sorting the rows would hide this # identity shift — the whole point of party-labeled data. germany <- rbind(   `2002` = c(CDU = 0.315, CSU = 0.096, SPD = 0.416,              FDP = 0.078, Gruene = 0.091, Linke = 0.003),   `2005` = c(CDU = 0.293, CSU = 0.075, SPD = 0.362,              FDP = 0.099, Gruene = 0.083, Linke = 0.088),   `2009` = c(CDU = 0.312, CSU = 0.072, SPD = 0.235,              FDP = 0.150, Gruene = 0.109, Linke = 0.122) ) alpha_beta_gamma(germany, q = c(0, 1, 2, 5)) #> Warning: as_composition(): renormalizing rows so each sums to 1 (max deviation was 0.001). #> <beta_diversity: T = 3 rows, 4 q points> #>  q    alpha     beta    gamma T #>  0 6.000000 1.000000 6.000000 3 #>  1 4.689208 1.040449 4.878880 3 #>  2 3.997689 1.039583 4.155931 3 #>  5 3.221741 1.067642 3.439665 3

title: Diversity-profile crossings — crossings
text: Detects q values two diversity profiles D_a(q) D_b(q) change order — central notion "structural incomparability" paper. Two detection strategies available.

title: Diversity-profile crossings — crossings
code: crossings(   x,   y = NULL,   q = seq(0, 5, by = 0.05),   method = c("interp", "signchange"),   tol = 1e-12 )

title: Diversity-profile crossings — crossings
text: x one : numeric vector (requires non-null y), numeric matrix rows = systems, diversity_profile object. y optional second composition profile. supplied x single composition / profile, single pair scored. Ignored x multi-row. q grid q values evaluate profiles (x/y compositions rather profiles). Default seq(0, 5, 0.05). method one "interp" "signchange". tol numeric tolerance equality zero.

title: Diversity-profile crossings — crossings
text: S3 "crossings" list elements: pairs — data.frame columns , j, id_i, id_j, n_crossings, first_q. locations — named list numeric q* vectors, one per pair. profiles — underlying diversity_profile (plotting).

title: Diversity-profile crossings — crossings
text: "interp": linear interpolation grid points D_a(q) - D_b(q) changes sign. Precise crossing location; sensitive noise grid. "signchange": count sign changes non-zero difference sequence. Cheap robust; reports first crossing's grid location rather interpolated value.

what: Hill number of order q — hill_number
title: Hill number of order q — hill_number
text: Computes Hill number (true diversity) order q single composition vector. Handles limiting cases q = 0 (richness), q = 1 (exponential Shannon entropy), q = Inf (1 / max(p)) explicitly. Vectorized q: pass vector get back vector.

title: Hill number of order q — hill_number
code: hill_number(p, q)

title: Hill number of order q — hill_number
text: p numeric vector non-negative weights summing (roughly) 1. Zeros allowed ignored. q non-negative numeric scalar vector, order. q = Inf allowed.

title: Hill number of order q — hill_number
text: Numeric scalar (length(q) == 1) numeric vector length(q) entries.

title: Hill number of order q — hill_number
code: # Party-labeled composition — column order carries party identity, # NOT rank. The largest party is whichever entry is largest. germany_2009 <- c(CDU = 0.312, CSU = 0.072, SPD = 0.235,                   FDP = 0.150, Gruene = 0.109, Linke = 0.122) hill_number(germany_2009, 0)      # 6 nonzero parties #> [1] 6 hill_number(germany_2009, 1)      # exp(Shannon) #> [1] 5.343992 hill_number(germany_2009, 2)      # ENP = 1 / sum(p^2) #> [1] 4.830498 hill_number(germany_2009, Inf)    # 1 / max(p) #> [1] 3.205128 hill_number(germany_2009, c(0, 1, 2, Inf)) #> [1] 6.000000 5.343992 4.830498 3.205128

title: partyscape 0.1.0
text: First release. Implements three analytical tools accompanying paper: Diversity profiles via diversity_profile() / evenness_profile() / hill_number(). Profile crossings via crossings() (linear-interpolation sign-change detectors). Multiplicative beta diversity via alpha_beta_gamma() / collapse_to_elections(). Also: Scalar summaries: hhi(), rae(), enp() (alias effective_parties()), dominance_gap(), top_two(). Null model: taagepera_allik_shares(). ParlGov accessors: fetch_parlgov(), parlgov_seat_shares(). autoplot() / plot() / print() methods S3 class.
